query: Compare Medicaid versus provider manual guidance for dual eligible pharmacy claims.
answer: {"headline_summary": "The provider manual, with an effective date of October 1, 2025, is more recent and provides more detailed guidance on dual eligible pharmacy claims, particularly regarding Medicare enrollment requirements and payment limitations. Policy documents, with the newest relevant update from February 1, 2024, primarily refer providers to the manual for pharmacy claims guidance and reiterate that dual eligible members access immunization services through Medicare.", "policy_definition": "For pharmacy claims, providers should refer to the eMedNY New York State Billing Guidelines – Pharmacy and the eMedNY Prospective Drug Utilization Review/Electronic Claims Capture and Adjudication ProDUR/ECCA Provider Manual [New York State Medicaid Update April 2023 Volume 39 Number 8:9 — April 1, 2023]. Dual eligible members will continue to access immunization services through Medicare [New York State Medicaid Update August 2021 Volume 37 Number 10:9 — August 1, 2021]. Pharmacy providers must comply with NYS Medicaid policy for 340B drug claims as outlined in the NYRx the NY Medicaid Pharmacy Program – Pharmacy Manual Policy Guidelines and Medicaid Update articles [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:1 — February 1, 2024].", "provider_manual_definition": "The New York State (NYS) Medicaid program requires Medicare enrollment for pharmacies enrolling in Medicaid to ensure that Medicaid dual eligible members receive drugs and supplies from Medicaid providers enrolled in accordance with federal and State laws and regulations [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:21 — October 1, 2025]. All Medicaid enrolled pharmacy providers are required by federal law to submit claims and accept assignments for Medicare covered services provided to Medicaid dual eligible members, regardless of CMS-460 enrollment status [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:21, 22 — October 1, 2025]. The total Medicare/Medicaid payment to the pharmacy provider will not exceed the amount the pharmacy provider would have received for a Medicaid-only patient, and this payment must be accepted as payment in full [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:18 — October 1, 2025].", "similarities": ["Both sources indicate that dual eligible members access immunization services through Medicare [New York State Medicaid Update August 2021 Volume 37 Number 10:9 — August 1, 2021] and [New York State Medicaid Update August 2019 Volume 35 Number 9:1 — August 1, 2019]."], "differences": ["The provider manual explicitly details the requirement for Medicare enrollment for pharmacies serving dual eligible members, including different types of Medicare enrollment (CMS-460, CMS-855B, CMS-855S) [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:21, 22 — October 1, 2025], which is not detailed in the policy documents.", "The provider manual specifies that the total Medicare/Medicaid payment to the pharmacy provider for dual eligible members will not exceed the Medicaid-only fee and must be accepted as payment in full [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:18 — October 1, 2025], a detail not present in the policy documents.", "Policy documents refer providers to external billing guidelines and manuals for pharmacy claims [New York State Medicaid Update April 2023 Volume 39 Number 8:9 — April 1, 2023], whereas the provider manual itself contains specific requirements for dual eligible claims."], "caveats": null}

query: Compare Medicaid versus provider manual guidance for dual eligible pharmacy claims.
answer: {"headline_summary": "The provider manual, with an effective date of October 1, 2025, is more recent than the newest policy update from February 1, 2024, and therefore should receive more weight for current guidance on dual eligible pharmacy claims. The provider manual emphasizes mandatory Medicare enrollment for pharmacies serving dual eligible members and outlines payment limitations, while policy documents primarily address immunization services for dual eligibles.", "policy_definition": "Pharmacies are only able to bill for Medicaid non-dual eligible members, with dual eligible members continuing to access immunization services through Medicare [New York State Medicaid Update August 2021 Volume 37 Number 10:9 — August 01, 2021]. This guidance was reiterated in August 2019, stating that dual-eligible enrollees will continue to access immunization services through Medicare [New York State Medicaid Update August 2019 Volume 35 Number 9:1 — August 01, 2019]. For general pharmacy claims, providers should refer to the eMedNY New York State Billing Guidelines – Pharmacy and the eMedNY Prospective Drug Utilization Review/Electronic Claims Capture and Adjudication ProDUR/ECCA Provider Manual [New York State Medicaid Update April 2023 Volume 39 Number 8:9 — April 01, 2023]. Pharmacy providers must comply with NYS Medicaid policy for 340B drug claims as outlined in the NYRx the NY Medicaid Pharmacy Program – Pharmacy Manual Policy Guidelines and Medicaid Update articles [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:1 — February 01, 2024].", "provider_manual_definition": "All Medicaid enrolled pharmacy providers are required by federal law to submit claims and accept assignments for Medicare covered services provided to Medicaid dual eligible members, regardless of CMS-460 enrollment status [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:21, 22 — October 01, 2025]. The New York State (NYS) Medicaid program requires Medicare enrollment for pharmacies enrolling in Medicaid to ensure that Medicaid dual eligible members receive drugs and supplies from Medicaid providers enrolled in accordance with federal and State laws and regulations [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:21 — October 01, 2025]. The total Medicare/Medicaid payment to the pharmacy provider will not exceed the amount that the pharmacy provider would have received for a Medicaid-only patient, and if the Medicare payment is greater than the Medicaid fee, no additional payment will be made [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:18 — October 01, 2025]. The Medicare and Medicaid payment (if any) must be accepted as payment in full, and a pharmacy provider of a Medicare Part B benefit cannot seek to recover any Medicare Part B deductible or coinsurance amounts from Medicaid members [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:18 — October 01, 2025].", "similarities": ["Both sources acknowledge the role of Medicare for dual eligible members. The policy documents state that dual eligible members access immunization services through Medicare [New York State Medicaid Update August 2021 Volume 37 Number 10:9 — August 01, 2021], and the provider manual mandates that Medicaid enrolled pharmacies submit claims and accept assignments for Medicare covered services for dual eligible members [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:21, 22 — October 01, 2025]."], "differences": ["The provider manual explicitly details the requirement for Medicare enrollment for pharmacies serving dual eligible members and outlines payment limitations, stating that the total Medicare/Medicaid payment will not exceed the Medicaid-only fee and must be accepted as payment in full [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:18, 21, 22 — October 01, 2025]. In contrast, the policy documents primarily focus on immunization services for dual eligible members, stating they access these services through Medicare, and generally refer providers to other billing guidelines for pharmacy claims without detailing specific dual eligible payment rules [New York State Medicaid Update August 2021 Volume 37 Number 10:9 — August 01, 2021], [New York State Medicaid Update April 2023 Volume 39 Number 8:9 — April 01, 2023]."], "caveats": null}

query: How do Medicaid and provider manual guidance define primary payer responsibility for dual-eligible pharmacy claims?
answer: {"headline_summary": "The newest provider manual, effective October 1, 2025, provides more detailed guidance on primary payer responsibility for dual-eligible pharmacy claims, emphasizing that Medicare and Medicaid payments must be accepted as payment in full and that Medicaid is the payer of last resort.
